title: Itï؟½s Only A Way Heï؟½s Got
content:
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  (As sung by the camp fire.)


آ آ آ آ No doubt the sayingï؟½s all abroad,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And rattling through the land.
آ آ آ آ We hear it at the mangle, too,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ With ï؟½What are you going to stand?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m sure I donï؟½t know which to choose,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s really such a lotï؟½
آ آ آ آ But I hope my song youï؟½ll not refuse,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ For itï؟½s only a way Iï؟½ve got.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Tol, lol, litter, tol, lol.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Tol, lol, the rol, lay.

آ آ آ آ In Sydney town a gal I met,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Her dress was rather gay,
آ آ آ آ I think the place, it was Pitt Street,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Or somewhere near that way.
آ آ آ آ Says she, ï؟½The night is very cold,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Pray, stand a drop of Hot.
آ آ آ آ I hope my freedom youï؟½ll excuse,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ For itï؟½s only a way Iï؟½ve got.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Tol, lol, &c.

آ آ آ آ The drink we soon put out of sight,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And off for home did walk,
آ آ آ آ When a fellow came up and quite polite
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ To her began to talk.
آ آ آ آ He drew my ticker from my fob,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And bolted like a shot.
آ آ آ آ Says she, ï؟½Oh, take no notice, Bob,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s only a way heï؟½s got.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Tol, lol, &c.

آ آ آ آ Says I, ï؟½Iï؟½ll soon catch you, my chap,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And arter him I flies,
آ آ آ آ When another stepped up and knocked my hat
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Completely oï؟½er my eyes.
آ آ آ آ He from my pocket drew my purse,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And off with it did trot;
آ آ آ آ Says she, ï؟½Itï؟½s well it is no worse,
آ آ آ آ  But itï؟½s only a way heï؟½s got.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Tol, lol, &c.

آ آ آ آ A little further on we went.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I had got rather shy.
آ آ آ آ Then a butcher ran his tray
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Right bang into my eye.
آ آ آ آ The fellow said it was my fault,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Called me a drunken sot.
آ آ آ آ Then, like a thief, he slunk away,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ ï؟½Twas only a way heï؟½d got!

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Tol, lol, &c.

آ آ آ آ Now, as we walked along the street,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ A lot of chaps we met.
آ آ آ آ I saw they on a game were bent;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Says they, ï؟½How fat you get!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ I got from them some ugly pokes,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ They made me a regular Scot.
آ آ آ آ They said, ï؟½Oh, never mind our jokes,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s only a way weï؟½ve got!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Chorus: Tol, lol, &c.

آ آ آ آ I have grown tired of Sydney town
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Since Iï؟½ve lost all my cash,
آ آ آ آ And so will up the country go,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And tell them of my smash.
آ آ آ آ Oh, then weï؟½ll have such lots of fun,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll court Miss Polly Scott;
آ آ آ آ And if she asks me what I mean
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll tell her itï؟½s a way Iï؟½ve got.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Tol, lol, &c.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
